summary: Install-time system reconnaissance and exfiltration to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: Package ships an install-time exfiltration payload. package.json line 30 wires a lifecycle script that loads child_process, os, and https, collects host identifiers and process.env, and POSTs them to https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js (lines 4-9) imports fs, os, https, and child_process; lines 142-161 issue https.request/https.get calls to the same https://ddjidd564.github.io endpoint; lines 233-246 invoke execSync('whoami') and read os.hostname()/os.platform(); lines 93-217 walk filesystem paths via fs.existsSync. The combination of install-time hostname/whoami/env collection plus hardcoded outbound POST to an anonymous GitHub Pages host (not a publisher domain, no version pinning, no integrity check) is a credential/system-intel harvester. The package name 'crypto-credential-scanner' with this code shape indicates the scanner is aimed at the installer's environment, not at a target the user supplies.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
